name: Op-Ed: Why do so many countries have tariffs?
abstract: Despite their economic inefficiencies, tariffs are prevalent globally due to political realities and human nature. The "concentrated benefits and diffused costs" dynamic explains how small, organized groups gain significantly from tariffs, while dispersed costs make public opposition difficult. The "infant industry" argument, though seemingly logical, often leads to perpetual government dependence for protected industries. Additionally, anti-foreign bias and a zero-sum mindset among the public contribute to support for protectionist policies. The article argues that tariffs are a zero-sum policy in a positive-sum world, ultimately harming all parties, and are ineffective at addressing international concerns, emphasizing the historical benefits of free trade.
